name: Kakslauttanen Arctic Resort
description: Rent out a glass igloo to watch the Northern Lights like you live in the future.

name: Temppeliaukio Church
description: A subterranean house of worship carved right out of the bedrock.

name: The National Library of Finland
description: Architecturally stunning library serves as the hub of Finnish scholarly life and culture.

name: Götan Maailma
description: A lovingly curated curio shop in which the taxidermy wear newspaper party hats, and peanut shells are spread across every surface.

name: Kamppi Chapel
description: An alien church provides much needed silence in the midst of a Finland shopping center.

name: Kemi Snow Castle
description: This fantastical icy castle is built anew every winter.

name: Kummakivi Balancing Rock
description: Legend has this precariously placed rock was placed by giants. Science has another explanation.

name: Bad Bad Boy
description: This endearingly discomfited urinating statue commemorates the sneaky pees had by us all.

name: Fortress of Suomenlinna
description: This irregularly shaped sea fortress has defended not one but three different sovereign states.

name: Sibelius Monument
description: An abstract monument to a symphonic composer.

name: Seurasaari Open Air Museum
description: A small island dotted with old, historic Finnish buildings.

name: The Silent People
description: Menacing or in memoriam, the Silent People and their meaning are left to interpretation.

name: ‘Viisas Hiiri’ (‘Wise Mice’)
description: The tiny, studious rodents greet visitors to the National Archives of Finland.

name: Helsinki University Museum
description: A collection of collections ranging from dentistry to minerals.

name: The Moomin House
description: A replica of the home of a famous fictional troll family.

name: Orinoro Gorge
description: Hidden deep in a forest is this geological wonder carved by glaciers.

name: Pohjola Insurance Building
description: This insurance company building reminds customers that witches, bears, and gnomes are out there, best be insured.

name: Santa Claus's Main Post Office
description: Roughly 17 million letters to Santa have found their way to this Arctic Circle post office.

name: Treriksröset
description: This lake-bound geographical marker sits at the point where three European countries intersect.

name: Salla Reindeer Park
description: Quality time with reindeer in the stunning scenery of their natural Lappish habitat above the Arctic Circle.

name: Rovaniemi's Arctic Circle Border
description: If you visit Santa's village, you can get your passport stamped to show you crossed into the Arctic Circle.

name: Veijo Rönkkönen Sculpture Garden
description: Collection of nearly 500 concrete figures sculpted by the late Veijo Rönkkönen.

name: HaliPuu's Campfire Barista
description: In the Hugging Tree Forest, lingonberry marshmallows, hot chocolates, and the best campfire lattes north of the Arctic Circle come to you.

name: Högberget Cave
description: Crawling into this primordial cave may feel disconcertingly familiar.

name: Oranki Art
description: A little-known sculpture park of contemporary environmental art in a Lapland forest.

name: Helsinki Observatory
description: Finland's oldest astronomical observatory once played a crucial role in seafaring and timekeeping.

name: Siida Museum
description: A beautiful Arctic museum and nature center preserves the culture of the Sámi people.

name: Sompasauna
description: No matter how many times this community-run sauna gets destroyed, it always rises from the ashes.

name: Lake Bodom
description: Finnish lake, and scene of an infamous murder mystery.

name: Puijo Tower
description: A 246-foot-high revolving restaurant rises unexpectedly from a forested Finnish hilltop.

name: Pielpajärvi Wilderness Church
description: This disused church, left to the elements of the Arctic Circle's forests is open for exploration.

name: Abandoned Bunkers of Salpalinja
description: Fortifications built for a Soviet invasion that never came now sit abandoned in a losing battle against nature.

name: Kuappi
description: The smallest restaurant in the world.

name: Pukkisaari
description: On a small island, a replica of a Finnish village from the Iron Age.

name: Tree Mountain
description: This intricately spiralling forest was created as a monumental work of art.

name: Lyhdynkantajat (The Lantern Bearers)
description: These enormous, stern-faced statues greet visitors with lanterns, and sometimes costumes.

name: Treriksrøysa
description: This cairn marks the only place where Central European Time, Eastern European Time, and Further-eastern European Time meet.

name: Hailuoto Church
description: This modernist island church incorporates the Finnish nature into its worship practices.

name: Oulu Groke
description: This lonely rock has been turned into a character loved and feared by children.

name: Imatrankoski
description: These Finnish rapids have been attracting tourists for over a hundred years and are now released with the push of a button.

name: Tampere Cathedral Frescoes
description: The church's remarkable frescoes incorporate Finnish artist Hugh Simberg's trademark themes of death and the supernatural.

name: Asemapäällikönhovi Building
description: This building featuring Brutalist architecture has a rich media production history.

name: Heavy Metal Knitting World Championships
description: One of the world's most unusual events combines headbanging and needlework.

name: Bengtskar Lighthouse
description: The tallest lighthouse in the Nordic countries was also the site of a bloody World War II battle.

name: Lonna Island
description: In the waters outside Helsinki, a tiny island once used as a military outpost now welcomes visitors.

name: Posankka
description: Pigduck of Turku.

name: Astuvansalmi Rock Paintings
description: These 5,000-year-old cave paintings provide a glimpse into prehistoric Finnish life.

name: Lake Lappajärvi
description: Nuclear waste experts study this prehistoric crater lake to envision landscapes one million years in the future.

name: Villa Mehu
description: This abandoned villa of makeshift buildings was once the home of dancer Elis Sinistö.

name: Ranua Wildlife Park
description: Finland's northernmost zoo is home to the country's only polar bears.

name: Mattolaituri
description: This restaurant combines two Finnish traditions in one place: warm-weather relaxation and outdoor carpet-cleaning.

name: Lenin Museum
description: The last permanent exhibit dedicated to the Soviet leader.

name: HaliPuu Forest
description: A lumberjack in Finnish Lapland did not want to cut down his Arctic pines, so he offered them up to be adopted by nature-lovers.

name: Pike's Gut
description: The etchings on this narrow channel between two islands bear witness to maritime travelers who have passed through since the Middle Ages.

name: Arctic Guesthouse & Igloos
description: This bed and breakfast is steeped in history and offers guests a unique way to experience nature—glass igloos on a frozen lake.

name: Matti Lepistö Sculpture Garden
description: More than 200 eclectic sculptures surround the artist's home.

name: 'Oma maa mansikka'
description: This ten-ton fruit sculpture celebrates the Finland homeland.

name: Turku Cathedral
description: A cathedral resurrected from the ashes of one of Europe's greatest urban fires.

name: Pirunpesä (The Devil's Nest)
description: This honeycomb-textured tafone looks like a petrified troll head.

name: Bullet Holes in the National Museum of Finland
description: The door to the museum holds a unique artifact from the Finnish Civil War.

name: Jokelan Tiilitehdas Brick Factory
description: This once-mighty brick factory is now a unique museum experience.

name: Söderskär Lighthouse
description: A rare octagonal brick beacon on an isolated island in the Baltic.

name: Märket Lighthouse
description: A long-disputed lighthouse built on the wrong side of a Finnish/Swedish island.

name: National Memorial to the Winter War
description: Dedicated to the outnumbered Finnish forces who pushed back Soviet forces during the conflict.

name: Lapinlahti Hospital
description: This former hospital was once one of the oldest mental health facilities in Europe.

name: Lahti Ski Jump Tower
description: Every summer, an open-air swimming pool appears at the base of a Finnish city's beloved winter attraction.

name: Pronssikautinen Hauta
description: One of the oldest structures in Helsinki is this burrow grave that dates back to the Bronze Age.

name: Suomenlinna Church
description: This church also serves as a lighthouse complete with a beacon.

name: MiG-21 BIS on Verkkokauppa
description: Atop this electronic store resides a massive fighter jet.

name: Air Guitar World Championships
description: The grandest stage for the world's foremost masters of invisible instruments.

name: Alpo Koivumäki Safari Park
description: Extraordinary animals sculpted from leftover junk.

name: Valkea Mall Floor Clock
description: An incredibly durable timepiece glows beneath shoppers' feet.

name: 'Roots of the City'
description: Hundreds of signs pointing to the birthplaces of current Helsinki residents form stalactites on the ceiling of the cave-like Kamppi metro station.

name: Vitträsk Rock Paintings
description: These lakeside pictographs were some of the first pieces of prehistoric artwork found in Finland.

name: Futuro House No. 001
description: Visit Finnish architect Matti Suuronen's first UFO-shaped home.

name: Branch Boy Sculptures
description: A stick-sprouting statue watching a duo of disembodied dancing deities.

name: Paska Kaupunni
description: This misspelled graffiti reappears every time it is removed.

name: Elk Garden
description: A Finnish artist's unique fauna statuary.

name: Olavinlinna
description: This island castle now hosts a world-famous opera festival.

name: Krejansberget Bronze Age Burial Site
description: These large stone piles are ancient burial sites.

name: Rönnäs Archipelago Museum
description: Home to the finest collection of peasant boats in Finland.

name: Santa's Hometown
description: This Finnish town on the Arctic Circle is trademarked as the Official Hometown of Santa Claus.

name: Submarine Vesikko
description: The last surviving submarine from a strange chapter of Finnish history.

name: Old Bear Castles of Korkeasaari Zoo
description: These historical landmarks stand as reminders of the evolution of animal care and conservation.

name: Aerial Defence Memorial
description: An old anti-aircraft cannon.

name: Siilinkari Island
description: This island only appears in winter once the surrounding waters have frozen over.

name: Tytyri Mine
description: Descend 100 meters underground to explore Finland’s mining history.

name: Wasa Graffitilandia
description: Relocated graffiti- and street-art exhibition space in a former national railway warehouse.

name: Albert Edelfelt Studio Museum
description: See where this respected Finnish painter created over 200 works of art.

name: Pickala Soviet Triumphal Arch
description: A rare remnant of Finland’s Soviet-occupied past.

name: Kruunuvuori
description: Villas that once served as playgrounds for the rich now fall away in decay.

name: Tire Gorilla
description: A giant gorilla made of used car tires used to sit sentinel over a Helsinki college campus.

name: Wasa Graffitilandia
description: An abandoned amusement park turned street art gallery with open walls for visitors to spray paint.
